purpose: Public-safe status view for the first U.S. sector-regime depth wave inside OpenCompliance. It shows the current narrow overlap for HIPAA Privacy and Breach, GLBA and NYDFS, and CCPA/CPRA without pretending that the whole regulatory corpus is already mechanised.

programs.title: HIPAA Privacy and Breach mapping program

workstreams.title: Public-law and regulation decomposition
workstreams.summary: The current wave tracks 2 public-source frameworks and already has 2 with normalized public-control overlap.
workstreams.remainingWork: Widen the current narrow overlap into a fuller obligation graph without collapsing rights, notification, and documentary requirements into fake machine proof.

workstreams.title: Public-control normalization
workstreams.summary: 4 unique public controls currently overlap this program, with 4 already exercised by fixtures.
workstreams.remainingWork: Promote the next narrow controls only when their route, exact-anchor, and claim-schema boundaries are stable enough to stay honest.

workstreams.title: Reviewed exact-anchor corridor
workstreams.summary: The current wave records 4 reviewed exact-anchor entries and 0 candidate entries across the in-scope frameworks.
workstreams.remainingWork: Increase reviewed exact-anchor coverage while keeping gaps explicit and resisting family-proxy inflation.

workstreams.title: Typed claim coverage
workstreams.summary: The current overlap splits into 0 decidable, 4 attestation, and 0 judgment controls.
workstreams.remainingWork: Close the next typed evidence and attestation gaps before widening the public verifier surface for these regimes.

workstreams.title: Lean coverage
workstreams.summary: 0 currently overlapped controls are Lean-backed in the public corridor.
workstreams.remainingWork: Keep Lean usage narrow and technical: only widen it where the regime obligation really reduces to a decidable systems claim.

workstreams.title: Fixture rollout
workstreams.summary: 4 of 4 mapped controls currently appear in public fixture corridors.
workstreams.remainingWork: Turn the next rights, vendor, breach, and retention slices into replayable fixture-backed corridors instead of documentation-only backlog.

workstreams.title: External review
workstreams.summary: A dedicated sector-regime review program now defines the reviewer roles and pass gates for health, finance, and California privacy-law scrutiny.
workstreams.remainingWork: Run the review program with sector-specialist counsel, operators, and privacy engineers once the next control wave is ready.

publicNotes: This wave intentionally starts with HIPAA Privacy and Breach Notification rather than claiming the full HIPAA Security Rule technical corridor is already normalized.
A mapped control here means a narrow documentary or workflow slice, not that HIPAA compliance is fully mechanised or certified.

publicNotes: This wave tracks the first publication-safe overlap between the current OpenCompliance security controls and the public GLBA / NYDFS security baseline.
The current overlap is intentionally narrow: MFA, logging, encryption, service-provider terms, incident response, and retention/disposal governance.

Because the source law is public, reviewed anchors can be explicit statutory sections rather than blocker-only placeholders.
